skill.name: troubleshoot
skill.description: Guides structured troubleshooting for build, test, UI, dependency, Git, and runtime failures.
skill.summary: Diagnose common development failures and guide safe repair steps.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby backtick executions, path traversal, weak cryptography, and entropy issues. Review found the backtick detections are Markdown code fences and inline command examples, the path traversal is a documentation link, and no weak cryptography or prompt injection was present. The remaining risk is medium because the skill is designed to use Bash and recommends npm, test, build, and Git diagnostics that can execute project-defined scripts.

medium_findings.title: Troubleshooting Commands Can Execute Project Scripts
medium_findings.description: The skill allows Bash and recommends commands such as npm run build, npm test, npm install, npm ls, and git status. This is appropriate for troubleshooting, but npm lifecycle and project scripts can execute arbitrary code from the current project, so users should run them only in trusted repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and the skill declares Bash as an allowed tool. The commands are legitimate troubleshooting actions, but npm scripts and installs are a real execution risk in untrusted projects.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Detections Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across many SKILL.md lines. Manual review found Markdown fenced blocks, inline command names, tables, and example responses rather than executable Ruby source or dynamic shell interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is a Markdown instruction file, and the flagged spans are code fences or inline examples. No Ruby file, backtick expression, or user-controlled shell construction was found.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Link Is Not Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The ../../ sequence appears only in a Markdown link to docs/LSP_INTEGRATION.md. It does not read files, write files, or instruct the model to bypass workspace boundaries.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sequence is a passive documentation reference in Markdown. There is no file operation or command using the path.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And Entropy Indicators Are Unsupported
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings point to the skill description line, which contains natural language trigger text. No hash, cipher, encoded payload, binary blob, or encrypted content was found in the reviewed Markdown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are frontmatter and a natural language description. The high entropy score is plausibly caused by Japanese text and symbols, not packed or encrypted content.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to use Bash-based diagnostics and npm commands. These commands should be run with normal user confirmation and repository trust checks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in the allowed-tools frontmatter and command examples. Risk depends on the target repository and user approval flow.

content.value_statement: Development failures often produce unclear logs and scattered symptoms. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured checks, clear explanations, and cautious repair steps.
actual_capabilities: Guides environment checks with node, npm, Git status, build logs, test logs, and dependency summaries.
Classifies common failures such as missing modules, type errors, build failures, runtime issues, and environment problems.
Provides response patterns for automatic fixes, user confirmation, and escalation after repeated failures.
Explains troubleshooting workflows for blank screens, failed npm installs, broken UI actions, and Git conflicts.
Recommends LSP diagnostics, go-to-definition, and reference checks to trace code-level causes.
limitations: It is an instruction-only Markdown skill and does not include executable helper scripts.
Some guidance assumes JavaScript, Node.js, npm, Git, and LSP tooling are available.
Commands such as npm test or npm run build can execute project-defined scripts.
It cannot guarantee a fix for complex failures without project context and logs.

use_cases.title: Recover From Build Failures
use_cases.description: A developer can collect logs, identify likely causes, and choose a repair path after a failed build.

use_cases.title: Explain Errors To Non-Experts
use_cases.description: A product builder can receive plain-language explanations and clear next steps without reading stack traces alone.

use_cases.title: Triage UI And Runtime Bugs
use_cases.description: A support engineer can reproduce symptoms, inspect logs, and escalate unresolved issues with a concise report.

prompt_templates.title: Run A Basic Diagnosis
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for an unclear problem
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose why this project is not working. Start with safe read-only checks and explain each result in simple terms.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate A Build Error
prompt_templates.prompt: The build is failing. Review the latest build output, identify the most likely cause, and propose the smallest safe fix.

prompt_templates.title: Trace A Broken UI Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: A button or form is not working. Reproduce the issue, inspect relevant UI code and logs, then verify the behavior after fixing it.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare An Escalation Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a troubleshooting report with the symptom, evidence, commands run, attempted fixes, remaining risks, and recommended next action.

output_examples.input: The page is blank after deployment.
output_examples.output: The skill would summarize likely causes, inspect build and console evidence, identify the failing area, and suggest a focused fix.

output_examples.input: npm install fails with a dependency error.
output_examples.output: The skill would explain the dependency conflict, propose safe cleanup steps, and ask before making changes.
The skill would compare repeated symptoms, list previous attempts, and prepare an escalation report after repeated failures.

best_practices: Start with read-only diagnostics before running repair commands.
Ask for confirmation before dependency installs, configuration changes, or destructive operations.
Record symptoms, evidence, attempted fixes, and verification results for future debugging.
anti_patterns: Do not run npm scripts in untrusted repositories without reviewing package scripts first.
Do not make broad dependency or configuration changes before identifying the likely cause.
Do not continue automatic repair attempts after repeated failures without escalation.

faq.question: What kinds of problems does this skill handle?
faq.answer: It covers build errors, test failures, dependency issues, Git conflicts, blank screens, broken UI flows, and runtime errors.

faq.question: Does this skill execute fixes automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide an agent toward fixes, but it instructs confirmation before risky or destructive changes.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses Bash-oriented troubleshooting and npm commands. These are useful, but they can execute project-defined code.

faq.question: Is this skill specific to JavaScript projects?
faq.answer: Much of the guidance targets Node.js, npm, Git, and LSP workflows, so JavaScript projects are the best fit.

faq.question: Can it help non-technical users?
faq.answer: Yes. The response patterns emphasize plain-language explanations, clear choices, and escalation when a problem is complex.

faq.question: What evidence should users provide?
faq.answer: Useful evidence includes the symptom, recent changes, error messages, screenshots, console logs, build output, and reproduction steps.
